sync.explainTitle: How does sync work? (the full story)

what.title: What it is
what.body: Sync keeps your Cardo data — to-dos, notes, calendar, habits, settings, assistants and their chats — identical across your devices. It is optional and off by default. Without sync, Cardo remains 100% offline.

key.title: The key
key.body: Everything is protected by one key (CRD1-…) that YOU generate on your first device. It is stored in the operating system's keychain, never in a file, and never sent anywhere. Anyone who has this key can read your synced data — treat it exactly like a password. The join switch controls whether the key can currently be shown for adding new devices; devices that already joined stay in the group even when it is off.

crypto.title: The encryption
crypto.body: Before anything leaves your device, it is end-to-end encrypted (XChaCha20-Poly1305). From your key, two separate keys are derived: one that a storage service may see for authentication, and the data key that never leaves your device. The result: whatever carries your data — Google, your NAS, a folder — only ever sees unreadable blocks and can neither read nor forge your data.

transports.title: The transports
transports.body: You choose where the encrypted blocks travel: your own Google Drive (Cardo only uses its hidden app folder and cannot see your files), any synced folder (iCloud, Dropbox, Syncthing, a network share), or a WebDAV server such as Nextcloud. Cardo runs a sync round every 5 minutes while enabled, plus whenever you press 'Sync now'. Conflicts resolve per field: the newest change wins on every device identically.

devices.title: Devices, joining, removing
devices.body: A key has 10 device slots. The device that created the key manages the group: it sees every device and can remove any of them — a removed device stops syncing on its next round and shows a notice. Deleting the key on the managing device dissolves the whole group. Every device always keeps its full local copy of the data.

limits.title: Honest limits
limits.body: Removal relies on devices honoring the removal record — Cardo devices do. But a device that keeps the key could technically still read new blocks in the storage. If a device is stolen or you don't trust it anymore: delete this key, create a fresh one and re-join your devices. Also: sync is near-real-time (minutes), not instant.

loss.title: If everything is lost
loss.body: There is no recovery service, no reset — by design, because nobody but you holds the key. If you lose every device AND the key, the encrypted data in the storage is permanently unreadable. Your local data on any surviving device is never affected.

sync.enableNeedsFolder: First choose a sync folder below — then sync can be switched on.
sync.joinDeniedBody: This sync group does not admit new devices right now. Ask the managing device to enable joining, then try 'Sync now' again — your key stays saved.

onboarding.restartHint: You can restart this tour any time in Settings → Help.

welcome.title: Welcome to Cardo!
welcome.body: Cardo is your pivot point: one calm place where you collect and arrange everything – exactly the way you want. This short tour shows you around. You can skip it at any time.

edit.title: Edit mode
edit.body: This button (or Cmd/Ctrl+E) unlocks the canvas: move widgets, resize them, add new ones and create more dashboard pages.

settings.title: Settings & self-test
settings.body: Themes, language, your profile – and the "Run self-test" button that checks the whole app and gives you a report.

market.title: The tool market
market.body: Switch tools on and off here. The privacy light shows for every tool what stays local (green) and what would contact the internet (yellow) – explained in plain language.

palette.title: The command palette
palette.body: Press Cmd/Ctrl+K anywhere to search and run any command – create a task, start the timer, everything is one keystroke away.

privacy.title: Your data stays yours
privacy.body: Cardo works 100% offline. No account, no cloud, no telemetry. Everything lives in one local database and in plain files you own.

sync.title: Your data on every device
sync.body: Optional: Settings → Sync generates a key that stays in your keychain. Everything is end-to-end encrypted before it travels via your own Drive, folder or server. Off by default — Cardo stays 100% offline until you say otherwise.

templates.title: How would you like to start?
templates.subtitle: Pick a starter layout – everything stays freely rearrangeable. Or start with a blank canvas.

work.title: Work
work.body: Today, tasks, calendar, pomodoro and time tracking.

private.title: Home & life
private.body: Today, routine, habits, countdowns and notes.

focus.title: Deep focus
focus.body: Pomodoro, task board and your productivity stats.

clock.name: Clock
clock.description: A simple clock widget – the first proof that widgets work.

tour.title: This is a widget
tour.body: Every tool lives in a widget like this. In edit mode you can move and resize it.

help.how: Shows the current time in five styles: Digital, Analog, Minimal, In words (“half past nine”) and Terminal. Switch the style via the widget’s variant selector in edit mode.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: A time anchor on your dashboard — especially in fullscreen or on a second monitor.

counter.name: Counter
counter.description: Counts clicks and remembers them – the first proof that storage works.

tour.title: Widgets remember
tour.body: This counter survives restarts. Everything you do in Cardo is stored locally on your device.

help.how: A button and a number: every click counts up by one, and the count survives restarts because it is stored locally. Reset it whenever you want to start over.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: Counting anything by hand: cups of coffee, sets at the gym, cold calls, laps.

alarm.name: Alarm clock
alarm.description: Set alarms that notify you and play a chime – fully local.
alarm.privacy: Works fully offline. Alarm times and messages never leave this device.

tour.title: Your alarms
tour.body: Add an alarm with a time and a message. Cardo notifies you and plays a chime – everything stays on your device.

help.how: Set a time and an optional message — at that moment Cardo notifies you and plays a chime. Alarms can be switched on and off individually and deleted when you no longer need them. Everything stays on this device.
help.setup: No setup needed. Allow notifications for Cardo in your system settings so alarms reach you even when the app is in the background.
help.bestFor: Fixed anchors in your day: taking a break, leaving for the bus, that one call at 3 pm.

countdown.name: Countdown
countdown.description: Counts the days until your important events.
countdown.privacy: Works fully offline. Your events never leave this device.
countdown.empty: No countdowns yet. Add an event below.

test.storageRoundtrip: Countdowns can be stored and removed

tour.title: Your countdowns
tour.body: Add an event with a date and Cardo shows you how many days are left – updated daily, fully local.

help.how: Add an event with a date and Cardo counts the days until it arrives — and tells you when it is today. The Card, Big number and Ring variants range from a compact list to one giant number.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: Anticipation made visible: vacations, birthdays, launches, exam dates.

calendar.name: Calendar
calendar.description: Your own appointments with reminders – fully local, at a glance.
calendar.privacy: Works fully offline. Your appointments never leave this device.

tour.title: Your calendar
tour.body: Click a day to see its appointments and add new ones. Appointments with a time remind you shortly before they start.

help.how: A fully local calendar for your own appointments: pick a day in the month view, add an entry with a time or as all-day, and delete it once it is over. Upcoming appointments can remind you with a system notification.
help.setup: No setup needed. Allow notifications for Cardo in your system settings if you want appointment reminders.
help.bestFor: Private appointments and reminders you would rather keep off any cloud calendar.

notes.name: Notes
notes.description: Markdown notes as real .md files – edit, preview, keep forever.
notes.privacy: Works fully offline. Your notes are plain Markdown (.md) files in a folder you own – readable with any editor, no lock-in.

test.fileRoundtrip: Note files can be written, read and deleted

tour.title: Your notes, your files
tour.body: Every note is a real Markdown file in a folder on your device. Move, back up or open them with any other app – Cardo never locks them in.

help.how: Your notes are real Markdown (.md) files in a folder on your disk — no database, no lock-in, readable with any editor. Create, rename and delete notes in the list pane and write in the editor with a Markdown preview. Everything saves straight back to the files.
help.setup: Cardo uses a notes folder it sets up for you; via “Change folder” in the footer you can point it at any folder you like — for example one another app already syncs.
help.bestFor: Notes that should outlive any app: journals, reference material, meeting notes kept as plain files.

files-explorer.name: Files
files-explorer.description: Browse and edit the files in your Cardo folder – Markdown, text, images, PDF & HTML, Obsidian-style with wiki-links and backlinks.
files-explorer.privacy: Reads and writes files only inside your own Cardo folder. Everything stays on your device; nothing is uploaded.

help.how: Browses the files inside your Cardo folder: edit Markdown and text right in the widget, preview images, PDFs and HTML pages. Obsidian-style [[wiki-links]] connect your notes, and a backlinks panel shows which files link to the one you are viewing. Anything Cardo cannot display opens in your system’s default app.
help.setup: No setup — it works on your Cardo folder from the start. Everything stays in that folder on your device.
help.bestFor: Tending a local knowledge base: linked notes, reference PDFs and project files in one place.

pomodoro.name: Pomodoro
pomodoro.description: A fully customizable Pomodoro timer: focused work sessions, short breaks, and a long break after every set of cycles.
pomodoro.privacy: Works fully offline. Your timer state and settings never leave this device.

notify.title: Pomodoro
notify.workDone: Focus session finished – time for a break!
notify.shortBreakDone: Break is over – ready for the next focus session?
notify.longBreakDone: Long break is over – time to get back to it!

settings.sound: Play sound when a phase ends

test.phaseAdvance: Phases advance in the correct order

tour.title: Stay focused
tour.body: Start a focus session and Cardo counts down for you. After each session you earn a break – after a full set of cycles, a long one.
tour.settingsBody: Tap the gear to adjust session and break lengths, how many cycles lead to a long break, and whether a chime plays.

help.how: The classic focus rhythm: a work session, a short break, and after a set number of cycles a long break. Start, pause, reset or skip a phase anytime; when a phase ends, Cardo notifies you and can play a sound. The cycle counter shows where you are in the set.
help.setup: Works out of the box with the classic 25/5 rhythm. Open the timer settings (gear) to change focus length, break lengths, cycles until the long break, and the end-of-phase sound.
help.bestFor: Deep work in slices: writing, studying, coding — whenever enforced breaks keep you fresh.

quickcapture.name: Quick capture
quickcapture.description: Capture a thought instantly, sort it later – your GTD inbox.
quickcapture.empty: Inbox empty. Whatever crosses your mind – drop it here and sort it later.

toast.empty: Nothing to capture – the text is empty.

test.itemRoundtrip: Inbox item can be added, read and deleted

tour.title: Your inbox for everything
tour.body: Type a thought and press Enter – it lands here instantly. Later, send it to your To-Do list with one click or delete it. A global shortcut brings this input up from anywhere.

help.how: One input field, zero friction: type a thought, press Enter, it lands in your inbox. Later you send each item on to To-Do or delete it — capture now, sort later. The global shortcut Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Space focuses the input from anywhere, even while you are in another app.
help.setup: No setup needed — the global shortcut is registered automatically when the tool is active.
help.bestFor: The GTD-style inbox: catching fleeting thoughts mid-task without losing your focus.

routine.name: Daily routine
routine.description: Your daily checklist – starts fresh every morning, all data stays on this device.

test.itemRoundtrip: Routine items can be stored and deleted

tour.title: Your daily routine
tour.body: Check off your routine here every day. At midnight the list starts fresh – and everything stays on this device.

help.how: A checklist that resets itself every morning: add your recurring items once, tick them off during the day, and tomorrow they are all fresh again. When everything is checked, the widget celebrates with you.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: Morning and evening routines, daily start-of-work rituals — anything you do every single day.

stats.name: Statistics
stats.description: Your productivity at a glance: completed tasks, work time and pomodoros per day, week or month.
stats.empty: Nothing tracked in this range yet. Complete a task, run the work clock or finish a pomodoro – your numbers will show up here.

tour.title: Your numbers
tour.body: See what you've accomplished: tasks, work time and pomodoros – per day, week or month. Everything is counted locally on this device.

help.how: Pulls your numbers from the other tools on this device — tasks completed (To-Do), tracked work time (Work Clock) and finished pomodoros — and shows them per day, week or month. The Overview, Heatmap and Numbers variants present the same data as a chart, a calendar heatmap or plain figures.
help.setup: No setup — the stats fill up by themselves once you complete tasks, run the work clock or finish pomodoros.
help.bestFor: Weekly reviews and spotting patterns: which days you get things done, and where your time actually goes.

todo.description: Tasks with lists, priorities, categories and due dates – fully local.

test.queryByList: Tasks can be filtered by list
test.boardStatus: Board status stays in sync with the done flag
test.queryToday: Today query returns correct counts and ordering

intro.title: Your tasks, your lists
intro.body: Type a task and press Enter to add it. The colored dot shows its priority, and overdue due dates turn red.

manage.title: Organize with lists
manage.body: Switch lists with the tabs, create new ones with "+" and rename the active one. Completed tasks collect at the bottom until you clear them.

board.title: Your tasks as a board
board.body: Drag cards between To do, In progress and Done. Dropping a card on Done completes the task – just like ticking its checkbox in the list.

help.how: Type a task and hit Enter — done. Organize tasks into lists (plus the built-in Inbox), give them a priority (low/medium/high) and tick them off; completed tasks collect in their own section that you can clear in one go. The separate Board widget shows the same tasks as a To do / In progress / Done kanban.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: Everyday task management — from a simple groceries list to a per-project kanban board.

workclock.name: Workclock
workclock.description: Tracks your productive time per day – start, stop, done.
workclock.privacy: Works fully offline. Your times never leave this device.

tour.title: Your workclock
tour.body: Hit start when you begin working and stop when you take a break. Cardo adds up your productive time per day – everything stays on your device.

help.how: One button: start when you begin productive work, stop when you are done. Cardo sums your tracked time per day and shows the running weekly total. In the settings you can couple it with the Pomodoro timer so only real focus phases count.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: An honest answer to “how much did I actually work today?” — freelancing, home office, study sessions.

habits.name: Habits
habits.description: Track your daily habits with streaks and a 26-week heatmap – fully local.

widget.heading: Today
widget.allDone: All habits done – keep the streak alive!
widget.empty: No habits yet. Add your first one below.

test.habitRoundtrip: Habits can be stored and deleted

tour.title: Your habits
tour.body: Check off your habits every day and watch your streaks grow. The heatmap shows your last 26 weeks – and everything stays on this device.

help.how: Add the habits you want to build and check them off once per day. Cardo tracks your current streak per habit and draws a 26-week heatmap of your consistency. The List, Week grid and Streaks variants each highlight a different view of the same data.
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: Building daily habits — exercise, reading, practicing — wherever an unbroken streak is the motivation.

today.name: Today
today.description: Your day at a glance – events, tasks, routine and habits from your other tools.
today.privacy: Works fully offline. Only shows data other Cardo tools on this device expose via commands.

widget.emptyHint: Today shows your day at a glance as soon as To-Do, Calendar, Routine or Habits are active.

tour.title: Your day at a glance
tour.body: Today gathers events, tasks, routine and habits from your other tools – always via the command API, never by touching their data directly.

help.how: Today gathers your day from the other tools on this board: appointments from Calendar, due tasks from To-Do, your Routine checklist and today’s Habits — all in one glance. You can tick tasks off right here. The Standard, Compact and Agenda variants trade detail for space.
help.setup: No setup of its own — it fills up as soon as To-Do, Calendar, Routine or Habits are active. Without them it stays empty.
help.bestFor: The morning overview: one place that answers “what’s on today?” before you dive in.

weather.name: Weather
weather.description: Current weather and a 5-day forecast for your place – with honest data age.

privacy.summary: This tool contacts the free Open-Meteo weather service. Only two things ever leave your device: the city name you type into the search, and the coordinates of the place you picked. Never your name, your files, or anything else about you.
privacy.geo: Only the city name you type into the search field – nothing else.
privacy.forecast: Only the coordinates of your chosen place – never anything about you.

weather.setupHint: Which place's weather do you want? Search for a city to get started.
weather.noResults: No places found. Try a different spelling.
weather.searchError: Couldn't reach the place search right now. Check your connection and try again.
weather.noData: Offline – no weather data yet. Cardo will try again.

msg.offline: No connection right now – keeping the last weather data.

test.cacheRoundtrip: Weather cache can be stored and removed
test.wmoMapping: Weather codes map to the right icons

tour.title: Weather at a glance
tour.body: Search for your city once and Cardo shows the current weather plus five days ahead. You always see how old the data is – and offline, the last forecast simply stays put.

help.how: Shows the current weather and a 5-day forecast for a place you pick, including wind and humidity — and is honest about how old the data is. Data comes from the free Open-Meteo service and is cached, so you still see the last state when offline. Full, Right now and Week variants fit different widget sizes.
help.setup: Search for your city once — that’s it. This tool goes online: only the city name you type and the coordinates of your chosen place are ever sent to Open-Meteo, nothing about you.
help.bestFor: The glance out of the window, extended: do I need a jacket today, and what does the week look like?

hydration.name: Hydration
hydration.description: Counts your daily glasses of water and reminds you to drink – fully local.
hydration.privacy: Works fully offline. Your drinking habits never leave this device.

notification.title: Time for a glass of water 💧
notification.body: You're at {{glasses}} of {{goal}} glasses – have a sip!

test.dayRoundtrip: Daily glass count is stored per day
test.windowLogic: Reminder time window is computed correctly

tour.title: Stay hydrated
tour.body: Tap +1 for every glass you drink. Cardo reminds you during your chosen hours until you hit your goal – everything stays on this device.

help.how: Every glass of water is one click; the widget shows your progress toward the daily goal and celebrates when you reach it. Optionally Cardo reminds you to drink at an interval you choose, within a time window (say 9:00–18:00).
help.setup: Works immediately. In the settings (gear) you set your daily goal and the reminder interval — 0 switches reminders off; allow notifications for Cardo if you use them.
help.bestFor: Drinking enough on desk days — when you only notice at 4 pm that you have had one coffee and no water.

assistant.name: Assistant
assistant.description: Your local thinking partner: dump your thoughts and turn them into concrete actions with one click – the AI runs entirely on your device.

privacy.summary: The AI runs entirely on your device – your thoughts and data never leave it. Only when you press install does Cardo download a model file from huggingface.co.
privacy.download: Downloads the selected AI model file from huggingface.co – only when you press install. Nothing about you is sent; the request only reveals which model is being fetched.

test.proposalValidate: Proposals are validated, hostile input is filtered
test.profileCrud: Profiles can be created, read and deleted
test.routerParse: Router answers resolve to a team member
test.templateKnown: Every model uses a known prompt template
test.catalogSane: Model catalog entries are complete and valid
test.modelCompetences: Model competences resolve for every model (en/de)

tour.title: Braindump instead of busywork
tour.body: Just write down whatever is on your mind. Your assistant turns it into concrete proposals – you only confirm with Yes, Edit or No. Everything runs locally.

help.how: Dump your thoughts into the input — the assistant reads your braindump and answers with a reply plus proposal cards, each one a concrete action like creating a to-do or an appointment. Nothing runs until you confirm a card with Yes; you can also edit or dismiss it. The AI model runs entirely on your device, and the assistant can remember facts you tell it. You can create several assistants with their own personality, and even teams that hand tasks to whoever is best at them.
help.setup: Open Settings → Assistant and follow the wizard: it checks your hardware, recommends a model and downloads it from huggingface.co — that download is the only network traffic of the local models. Optionally you can use Claude cloud models instead; those need the Claude Code CLI, a subscription and internet, and your chat content is then sent to Anthropic.
help.bestFor: Turning messy thoughts into concrete tasks, appointments and notes — and driving your whole dashboard in plain language.

eisenhower.name: Eisenhower Matrix
eisenhower.description: Sort tasks into four urgent/important quadrants and always know what to do next.
eisenhower.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

shared-expenses.description: Track group expenses, see who owes whom, and settle up with the fewest transfers.
shared-expenses.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

command.addDesc: Adds an expense to a group (created on demand): who paid, how much, what for, and optionally who shares it (comma-separated; default: all members).
command.contextDesc: Summarizes all groups with their open balances (who is owed, who owes).

widget.emptyGroups: Create a group with its members to get started.

test.balance-math: Cent-exact splits, balances sum to zero

tour.title: Shared Expenses
tour.body: Log group expenses and let Cardo work out who owes whom – settle up with the fewest transfers.

help.how: Create a group with its members, then log each expense with who paid, the amount and (optionally) who took part. Cardo keeps a running balance of who owes whom and computes a settlement plan with the fewest possible transfers, which you tick off as people pay up.
help.setup: Create a group and list its members, comma-separated — that is the whole setup.
help.bestFor: Flat shares, trips with friends, team lunches — everywhere “I’ll get this one” needs a fair reckoning later.

calculator.name: Calculator
calculator.description: Expression calculator with functions, deg/rad trig modes and a history – no eval, ever.
calculator.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

command.calcDesc: Evaluates a math expression (e.g. "2+3*4", "sin(90)", "sqrt(2)^2") and returns the result – also usable by the assistant to answer math questions.
command.contextDesc: Returns the last calculations from the history.

error.math: Result is not a finite number

test.parser: Parser handles precedence, functions and errors
test.calc-command: Calc command computes and records history
test.history-crud: History storage roundtrip with cap works

tour.title: Calculator
tour.body: Type an expression or use the keypad – results land in the history, and the assistant can calculate for you too.

help.how: Type a full expression — 2+3*4, sin(90), sqrt(2) — and get the result instantly from a safe built-in parser (no eval, ever). Trig works in degrees or radians via a toggle, and the history keeps past calculations ready to load back into the input. Basic, Scientific and Compact variants change the layout.
help.setup: Works immediately; default angle mode, thousands separator and history length live in the settings (gear).
help.bestFor: Quick math without leaving your dashboard: bills, conversions in your head made checkable, homework spot-checks.

color-tool.name: Colors & Palettes
color-tool.description: Pick colors, build harmonies, save palettes and check WCAG contrast.
color-tool.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

command.contrastDesc: Computes the WCAG contrast ratio of two colors (hex/rgb/hsl) with an AA/AAA verdict for normal and large text.
command.savePaletteDesc: Saves a named palette from a comma-separated color list (hex/rgb/hsl).
command.contextDesc: Lists the saved palettes with their color counts.

msg.invalidColor: Could not parse one of the colors.
msg.invalidColors: The color list contains an invalid entry.

tour.title: Colors & Palettes
tour.body: Pick a color, explore harmonies, save palettes – and check text contrast against WCAG right away.

help.how: Pick a base color and build harmonies from it — complementary, analogous, triadic or shades — then save the results as named palettes and copy any hex value with a click. The Contrast variant checks two colors against the WCAG thresholds (AA/AAA, normal and large text).
help.setup: No setup needed — drag the widget onto your board and go.
help.bestFor: Design work big and small: slides, websites, themes — and making sure your text stays readable.

snippets.name: Code Snippets
snippets.description: Store, search and copy code snippets with lightweight syntax highlighting for 8 languages.
snippets.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

command.addDesc: Saves a code snippet with title, language, body and optional comma-separated tags.
command.contextDesc: Reports how many snippets exist and names the newest ones.

widget.empty: No snippets yet – add your first one.

test.highlight-logic: Highlighter separates code, strings and comments

tour.title: Code Snippets
tour.body: Your local snippet library: search, expand, copy – with syntax highlighting and tags.

help.how: Store code snippets with a title, language and tags; lightweight syntax highlighting covers 8 languages. Full-text search and the tag filter find any snippet in seconds, and the copy button puts it on your clipboard.
help.setup: Works immediately; code font size and line wrapping live in the settings (gear).
help.bestFor: The commands and boilerplate you keep re-googling: shell one-liners, config blocks, SQL queries.

currency.name: Currency Converter
currency.description: Convert between ~30 currencies with daily-cached exchange rates – honest about their age, fully usable offline.

privacy.summary: This tool contacts the free open.er-api.com exchange-rate service at most once per day. The only thing that ever leaves your device is the three-letter code of your base currency (e.g. "EUR") in the request URL – never an amount, never anything personal. All conversions run against the locally cached table.
privacy.rates: Only the three-letter code of your base currency (e.g. "EUR") – never amounts, never anything about you.

currency.unknownCode: This currency is not in the cached table.
currency.noRates: No rates yet – Cardo fetches them once you're online.
currency.noPairs: No favorite pairs yet – add some via ⚙.

command.convertDesc: Converts an amount between two ISO currency codes (e.g. 100 EUR to USD) using the locally cached daily rates – works offline, never contacts the network.
command.refreshDesc: Fetches today's exchange rates for the base currency now. Offline, the cached table simply stays in place.
command.contextDesc: Summarizes the cached rate table, its age and the last conversion for the assistant.

msg.offline: No connection right now – keeping the cached rates.
msg.noRatesYet: No rates cached yet – run "Refresh exchange rates" once while online.
msg.unknownCode: Unknown currency code – use ISO codes like EUR, USD, GBP.

test.cacheRoundtrip: Rates cache can be stored and removed

tour.title: Currency at a glance
tour.body: Convert amounts, pin favorite pairs and read big single rates. Rates are fetched once a day and cached – you always see how old they are, and offline everything keeps working.

help.how: Converts between about 30 currencies using an exchange-rate table that is cached locally and refreshed at most once per day — the widget always tells you how old its rates are, and conversions keep working offline against the cache. Converter, Rate board and Single pair variants suit different needs.
help.setup: Pick your base currency in the settings (gear). This tool goes online: at most once a day it contacts open.er-api.com, and the only thing sent is the three-letter code of your base currency — never amounts, never anything personal.
help.bestFor: Travel budgets, shopping in foreign stores, invoices in other currencies — quick conversions with honest data age.

rss.name: RSS Reader
rss.description: Follow your favorite RSS and Atom feeds – unread counts, reading-list handoff, and everything cached for offline reading.

privacy.summary: This tool contacts exactly the feed servers YOU add – no more, no less. Each request is a plain download of that feed's address; nothing about you, your dashboard or your reading behavior is ever sent along. Fetched items are cached locally, so reading works offline.
privacy.feeds: Cardo contacts only the feed URLs you add yourself and sends nothing but the plain request for that address – never anything about you.

command.addFeedDesc: Subscribes to an RSS/Atom feed by URL. The feed is fetched lazily afterwards, so this also works offline.
command.markReadDesc: Marks all articles as read, or only those of one feed when a feed title is given.
command.contextDesc: Summarizes subscribed feeds and unread counts for the assistant.

tour.title: Your feeds, your rules
tour.body: Add any RSS or Atom feed and Cardo keeps the latest articles cached locally. Unread items are bold, one click opens the article and marks it read – and offline you simply keep what's already there.

help.how: Paste the URLs of the RSS or Atom feeds you want to follow; Cardo fetches them, counts unread articles and lets you mark things read or send an article straight to your Reading list. Everything fetched is cached, so you can read offline. List, Cards and Headlines variants change the density.
help.setup: Add at least one feed URL — that is all. This tool goes online: it contacts exactly the feed servers you add, no others, and each request is a plain download of that address; nothing about you or your reading behavior is sent.
help.bestFor: Following blogs and news on your terms: a chronological feed you chose, instead of an algorithm.

soundscapes.name: Soundscapes
soundscapes.description: An ambience mixer with bundled loops – rain, white noise, brown noise, a stream – for focused work.

privacy.summary: Works fully local. All sound loops ship with Cardo; nothing is streamed, nothing leaves your device.

settings.autoWithPomodoro: Follow Pomodoro: play during work phases, pause for breaks

command.playDesc: Starts the ambience mix – or a single named track (rain, white-noise, brown-noise, stream). Without a track, the last mix resumes.
command.stopDesc: Stops all ambience playback. The mix itself is kept for the next play.
command.contextDesc: Reports which tracks are playing and at what master volume for the assistant.

test.mixerLogic: Mixer state machine and volume math work

tour.title: Your background sound
tour.body: Mix rain, noise and a stream into your personal ambience. Everything ships with Cardo – nothing is streamed. Optionally the sound follows your Pomodoro phases.

help.how: An ambience mixer with four bundled loops — rain, white noise, brown noise and a stream. Toggle any combination, set each track’s volume plus a master level, and let it play while you work. All sounds ship with Cardo: nothing is streamed, nothing goes online. It can even follow your Pomodoro: sound during focus phases, silence in breaks.
help.setup: No setup needed — press play. The Pomodoro coupling lives in the settings (gear).
help.bestFor: Masking noisy surroundings and settling into focus — open-plan offices, cafés, restless evenings.

When you vote, only the poll ID, your choice and an anonymous device hash are sent – one vote per installation, no name, no profile. Nothing is loaded until you press the button.

qwen3-0-6b.strengths: Tiny and loads in a flash; runs on virtually any machine.
qwen3-0-6b.weaknesses: Very limited understanding – anything beyond simple requests confuses it.
qwen3-0-6b.idealFor: Trying the assistant out, or as a quick router model.

qwen3-1-7b.strengths: Solid small all-rounder with decent German and reliable structured output.
qwen3-1-7b.weaknesses: Shallow reasoning; misses details in longer braindumps.
qwen3-1-7b.idealFor: Fast everyday commands on 8 GB machines.

qwen3-4b.strengths: Good all-rounder: solid German and dependable JSON output for its size.
qwen3-4b.weaknesses: Noticeably slower on 8 GB machines; complex requests can overwhelm it.
qwen3-4b.idealFor: The sensible default on most machines.

qwen3-8b.strengths: Strong all-rounder with good German and very reliable structured output.
qwen3-8b.weaknesses: Needs 16 GB RAM to feel smooth.
qwen3-8b.idealFor: Main assistant on a 16 GB Apple Silicon Mac.

qwen3-14b.strengths: Very capable all-rounder; handles long, messy braindumps well.
qwen3-14b.weaknesses: Big download, needs plenty of RAM and replies noticeably slower.
qwen3-14b.idealFor: Quality-first main profile on machines with 16 GB or more.

qwen3-30b-a3b.strengths: Mixture-of-experts: near-14B quality at the speed of a ~3B model.
qwen3-30b-a3b.weaknesses: Huge download, and the full weights must fit in RAM (~24 GB).
qwen3-30b-a3b.idealFor: The best quality/speed mix on 32 GB Apple Silicon.

phi-4-mini.strengths: Remarkably clever for its size, especially at logic and structure.
phi-4-mini.weaknesses: Leans towards English – German replies can sound stiff.
phi-4-mini.idealFor: Fast English-language tasks on small machines.

phi-4.strengths: Very smart in the 14B class; strong reasoning and clean output.
phi-4.weaknesses: Leans towards English; German is usable but not its strength.
phi-4.idealFor: Complex reasoning tasks, preferably in English.

mistral-7b.weaknesses: Older generation – weaker German and less precise than newer 7–8B models.
mistral-7b.idealFor: A dependable everyday model on mid-range hardware.

mistral-small-24b.strengths: Very strong across the board, close to much larger models.
mistral-small-24b.weaknesses: Needs about 20 GB RAM and is not fast.

granite-3-1-8b.strengths: Built for function calling – very reliable command and JSON output.
granite-3-1-8b.weaknesses: Plain, sober writing; not the most creative.
granite-3-1-8b.idealFor: Profiles that mostly execute commands with structured output.

deepseek-r1-7b.strengths: Strong step-by-step reasoning for tricky, logical problems.
deepseek-r1-7b.weaknesses: Thinks out loud before answering, which makes it slow (the visible reasoning is stripped automatically).
deepseek-r1-7b.idealFor: Puzzling tasks where quality matters more than speed.

llama-3-2-1b.strengths: Tiny and instant; surprisingly capable for its size.
llama-3-2-1b.weaknesses: Very limited depth; Meta license notice applies.
llama-3-2-1b.idealFor: Router duty and very simple commands on any machine.

llama-3-2-3b.strengths: Strong for its size and pleasantly quick.
llama-3-2-3b.weaknesses: Limited depth on complex requests; Meta license notice applies.
llama-3-2-3b.idealFor: A snappy assistant on 8 GB machines.

llama-3-1-8b.strengths: Strong all-rounder with broad knowledge and good multilingual skills.
llama-3-1-8b.weaknesses: Meta license notice applies; needs ~8 GB RAM.

gemma-3-4b.strengths: Outstanding German and writing quality for its size.
gemma-3-4b.weaknesses: You must accept Google's Gemma terms before downloading.

gemma-3-12b.strengths: Excellent German, natural style, very good multilingual writing.
gemma-3-12b.weaknesses: Needs ~11 GB RAM; Google's Gemma terms must be accepted.
gemma-3-12b.idealFor: A German-first main assistant on 16 GB machines.

gemma-3-27b.strengths: The best German writing quality in this catalog.
gemma-3-27b.weaknesses: Very high RAM needs (~22 GB), slow; Gemma terms must be accepted.
gemma-3-27b.idealFor: Maximum text quality on 32 GB machines.

claude-fable-5.strengths: The most capable Claude model – strongest on complex, multi-step and agentic work.
claude-fable-5.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-fable-5.idealFor: The hardest tasks, where quality matters more than speed.

claude-opus-4-8.strengths: Very strong all-round model with high quality across almost every kind of task.
claude-opus-4-8.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-opus-4-8.idealFor: Demanding everyday work that needs consistently high quality.

claude-sonnet-5.strengths: Fast and strong at the same time – handles everyday tasks quickly and reliably.
claude-sonnet-5.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-sonnet-5.idealFor: The brisk default for day-to-day work.

claude-haiku-4-5.strengths: The fastest Claude model – answers almost instantly.
claude-haiku-4-5.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-haiku-4-5.idealFor: Light tasks, short questions and quick commands.
